parsed_findings.impact: Remote attacker can induce the server to make arbitrary HTTP requests revealing internal services.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 3 validation only checks malformed string and reject always fails, so any host can be fetched.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call /fetch?url=http://internal.service/api to retrieve internal data once.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: User-supplied url passed to axios.get allows SSRF because validation is broken.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: Attacker supplies a serialized payload via the state cookie so the server executes arbitrary code when /resume runs.
parsed_findings.impact: Remote users can achieve arbitrary code execution by crafting a malicious pickle payload in the cookie.
parsed_findings.proof: The endpoint decodes the client-provided state cookie and immediately calls pickle.loads on it, granting full control to the cookie.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Set the state cookie to a base64 pickle payload and request /resume to trigger untrusted deserialization.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Untrusted cookie is base64-decoded and fed into pickle.loads, allowing attacker-controlled deserialization.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: user requests backup→app formats `cmd` with `name`→`os.system` executes entire string.
parsed_findings.impact: Remote command execution as the app user during backup request.
parsed_findings.proof: `cmd = f'tar -czf /tmp/{name}.tgz {src}'` runs via `os.system`, so a malicious `name` can append `; rm -rf /`.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send POST `/backup` with `name` like `legit;uname -a` to execute extra commands.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Using user-controlled `name` in `os.system` call let attackers inject shell operators.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: 1. Submit attacker-controlled url; 2. Server performs request.get on that url without additional checks.
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can reach internal-only services through the server’s proxying of avatar fetches.
parsed_findings.proof: Line 6 directly calls requests.get on request.json['url'] after only checking http prefix.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send POST /avatar/fetch with url=http://internal/admin and observe request succeeds.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Server fetches arbitrary URLs without host allowlist, enabling SSRF via user-controlled url.
